answer:
\boxed{C}  

Greenlandic was the first Inuit language to develop a distinct writing system, using a modified version of the Latin alphabet rather than a non-Latin script. In fact, none of the listed languages historically used a writing system not based on the Latin alphabet; however, Greenlandic had an early standardized orthography. The question’s premise is slightly misleading, but among the options, Greenlandic’s writing system was established earliest.
